Negative Comments: Since students are not classified into sections by their GWA, I can see students who are at an advantage, average, or below average. And because of that, sometimes, the instructor does not address the issue of having "below-average students" in her class and just "let us proceed to the next topic" without thinking she might leave other students behind. Although she asks if we had absorbed the topic, it is not enough if only fast learners will respond to her.  I hate to see instructors WHO DO NOT wrap up or summarize the topic at the end of the lesson. They just leave the students thinking "Ah, iyon na 'yon?" "Yon na pala yung topic?" "Ah, ano raw, tapos na pala?"  Furthermore, I HOPE that explanations can be done in TAGALOG because we are Filipino speakers and NOT English Speakers. Some students are having a hard time understanding the instructor's accent in speaking in English. More so, REFRAIN FROM JUST reading the PowerPoint presentation or the module (I observe it from time to time) and ELABORATE IT, RELATE IT TO OTHER DISCIPLINES, AND APPLY OTHER CONCEPTS BENEFICIAL TO THE AUDIENCE for the students to sync better with the lesson.  *I know that we are already college students and should now be able to possess skills that a University requires, however, this point of view is not for the sake of me but for other students who fail to adapt to the instructor's teaching methods. Cultural fit is my advantage, but how about others who do not? Can the instructor be more careful and address proper audience analysis including the students' background?   **Remedial is not the key, but rather finding a way for students to appreciate and be in touch with your subject.
